purpose: Valibot is a modular, type-safe schema library for TypeScript/JavaScript used to validate unknown data (forms, API payloads, config files) at runtime with static type inference. Its modular API supports tree-shaking for very small bundle sizes (starts <700 bytes).

public_api.name: object
public_api.summary: Creates an object schema from an entries record of schemas.

public_api.name: string
public_api.summary: Schema that validates a string value.

public_api.name: number
public_api.summary: Schema that validates a number value.

public_api.name: boolean
public_api.summary: Schema that validates a boolean value.

public_api.name: array
public_api.summary: Schema that validates an array of items matching the given schema.

public_api.name: pipe
public_api.summary: Composes a schema with validation/transformation actions (e.g. email, minLength).

public_api.name: parse
public_api.summary: Parses input against schema; throws ValiError on failure, returns typed output on success.

public_api.name: safeParse
public_api.summary: Non-exception-based parse returning a result object with success/issues.

public_api.name: is
public_api.summary: Type guard function that returns a boolean for whether input matches schema.

public_api.name: parser
public_api.summary: Returns a reusable parse function for the given schema.

public_api.name: safeParser
public_api.summary: Returns a reusable safeParse function for the given schema.

public_api.name: InferOutput
public_api.summary: Type utility that infers the output TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: InferInput
public_api.summary: Type utility that infers the input TypeScript type of a schema.

public_api.name: email
public_api.summary: Pipe action that validates a string is a valid email address.

public_api.name: minLength
public_api.summary: Pipe action that enforces a minimum length on strings/arrays.

public_api.name: maxLength
public_api.summary: Pipe action enforcing a maximum length.

public_api.name: optional
public_api.summary: Wraps a schema to also accept undefined.

public_api.name: nullable
public_api.summary: Wraps a schema to also accept null.

public_api.name: nullish
public_api.summary: Wraps a schema to also accept null or undefined.

public_api.name: union
public_api.summary: Schema that matches if the input satisfies any of the given schemas.

public_api.name: intersect
public_api.summary: Schema that requires input to satisfy all given schemas.

public_api.name: literal
public_api.summary: Schema that validates an exact literal value.

public_api.name: picklist
public_api.summary: Schema matching one of a given list of literal values.

public_api.name: enum
public_api.summary: Schema matching values from a TypeScript enum.

public_api.name: record
public_api.summary: Schema for objects with dynamic string keys and uniform value schema.

public_api.name: tuple
public_api.summary: Schema for fixed-length tuple arrays.

public_api.name: transform
public_api.summary: Pipe action that transforms the parsed value.

public_api.name: check
public_api.summary: Pipe action for custom boolean-returning validation.

public_api.name: ValiError
public_api.summary: Error class thrown by parse when validation fails; contains issues.

public_api.name: flatten
public_api.summary: Flattens issues into a nested/flat error structure for form display.

public_api.name: parseAsync
public_api.summary: Async version of parse for schemas containing async actions.

public_api.name: safeParseAsync
public_api.summary: Async version of safeParse.

usage_patterns.title: Define a schema with pipe actions
usage_patterns.body: Use v.object to describe the shape and v.pipe to compose a base schema (e.g. v.string()) with validation actions (v.email(), v.minLength(8)). Schemas are values; combine small functions instead of chained methods to enable tree-shaking.

usage_patterns.title: Parse vs safeParse vs is
usage_patterns.body: Use v.parse(schema, input) when you want exceptions (throws ValiError). Use v.safeParse for a result object with { success, output, issues } and no throw. Use v.is(schema, input) as a boolean type guard.

usage_patterns.title: Infer TypeScript types from schemas
usage_patterns.body: Derive static types using type T = v.InferOutput<typeof Schema> (and v.InferInput for input types). This avoids duplicating type definitions alongside runtime schemas.

usage_patterns.title: Async validation
usage_patterns.body: For schemas that need async checks or transforms, use the *Async variants (objectAsync, pipeAsync, checkAsync, parseAsync, safeParseAsync) from the Async API section.

usage_patterns.title: Modular imports for small bundles
usage_patterns.body: Import as `import * as v from 'valibot'` or import specific functions. Because each schema/action is its own function, bundlers tree-shake unused code, keeping bundle size minimal.

gotchas: v.parse throws a ValiError on invalid input; use v.safeParse if you do not want exceptions.
Validation actions like email() or minLength() are not methods on string() — they must be composed via v.pipe(v.string(), v.email(), ...).
For schemas containing async logic, you must use the *Async variants and await parseAsync/safeParseAsync; sync parse will not work.
Breaking API changes occurred at v0.31.0; older code/tutorials may use a different API (see migration guide 'To v0.31.0').
Type inference uses InferOutput/InferInput — they can differ when transforms change the type between input and output.

examples.title: Login schema with parse
